policyText: Effective immediately, a 25% tariff is imposed on all imported steel and steel-derivative products entering the country. This measure aims to protect domestic steel manufacturing, preserve industrial jobs, and reduce dependency on foreign steel suppliers. All steel imports — including raw steel, steel alloys, structural beams, steel pipe, rebar, and finished steel components — will be subject to the new duty rate at port of entry. Domestic steel producers are expected to increase output to meet demand previously filled by imports. The tariff applies to all trading partners without exception. The administration projects this will create approximately 15,000 new steelworking jobs within 18 months and generate $2.4 billion in annual tariff revenue. Critics warn the measure could raise consumer prices on automobiles, appliances, and construction materials by 8-20%, potentially triggering retaliatory tariffs from major trading partners including China, the EU, Japan, and South Korea. Small businesses in construction, manufacturing, and retail sectors may face significant cost increases. The policy includes no exemption process for downstream manufacturers or hardship provisions for affected industries.

beliefs: Trade policy must consider working families first.
We need safety nets before tariffs.
controversial_ideas: This tariff is a handout to steel executives, not workers.
npcs.perception: The community is deeply divided. I need to propose concrete relief measures.
npcs.current_plan: Introduce an emergency relief package for affected small businesses.

npcs.name: Luis Martinez
npcs.bio: Luis Martinez works as a economics student and is known around town for being progressive and idealistic.
beliefs: Free trade lifts all boats.
Tariffs are a hidden tax on consumers.
controversial_ideas: Every economics textbook says tariffs are a net loss — why are we doing this?
npcs.perception: Students are joining the protests. This is about our future.
npcs.current_plan: Lead a student coalition joining the community protests.

npcs.name: Yuki Yamamoto
npcs.bio: Yuki Yamamoto works as a engineering student and is known around town for being quiet but determined.
beliefs: Innovation requires global collaboration.
Students will inherit whatever mess policy creates.
controversial_ideas: If steel gets expensive, we should just 3D-print alternatives.
npcs.perception: Students are joining the protests. This is about our future.
npcs.current_plan: Lead a student coalition joining the community protests.

npcs.name: Olga Ivanova
npcs.bio: Olga Ivanova works as a retired steel union rep and is known around town for being outspoken and passionate.
beliefs: Unions built the middle class.
We fought for these steel jobs — tariffs help protect them.
controversial_ideas: Back in my day we didn't import steel and we were better for it.
npcs.perception: I've seen economic downturns before but this one feels different — more personal.
npcs.current_plan: Help organize the community food bank and mutual aid network.

npcs.name: Ahmed Ali
npcs.bio: Ahmed Ali works as a retired factory manager and is known around town for being cautious and pragmatic.
beliefs: Tariffs helped in the 80s but the world is different now.
Fixed incomes can't absorb price shocks.
controversial_ideas: Retirees are the forgotten casualties of trade wars.
npcs.perception: I've seen economic downturns before but this one feels different — more personal.
npcs.current_plan: Help organize the community food bank and mutual aid network.

npcs.name: Rosa Lopez
npcs.bio: Rosa Lopez works as a labor organizer and is known around town for being outspoken and passionate.
beliefs: Workers deserve living wages and job security.
Policy must center the most vulnerable.
controversial_ideas: A general strike is the only language corporations understand.
npcs.perception: The movement is strong but we need concrete demands, not just anger.
npcs.current_plan: Escalate to sustained protest actions and media outreach.

npcs.name: Omar Said
npcs.bio: Omar Said works as a environmental activist and is known around town for being progressive and idealistic.
beliefs: Steel tariffs will prop up dirty domestic furnaces.
Green alternatives need a chance to compete.
controversial_ideas: We should ban domestic coal-fired steel production entirely.
npcs.perception: The movement is strong but we need concrete demands, not just anger.
npcs.current_plan: Escalate to sustained protest actions and media outreach.

npcs.name: Anna Taylor
npcs.bio: Anna Taylor works as a soybean farmer and is known around town for being hardworking and stoic.
beliefs: Farmers always pay the price for trade wars.
Retaliatory tariffs will crush exports.
controversial_ideas: If China retaliates on soybeans, half the farms in this county go under.
npcs.perception: I'm talking to the bank about restructuring my farm loan. It's that bad.
npcs.current_plan: Negotiate with creditors and explore alternative income streams.

npcs.name: Jin Wei
npcs.bio: Jin Wei works as a dairy farmer and is known around town for being skeptical of government.
beliefs: Equipment costs are already crushing us.
Steel tariffs mean more expensive tractors and silos.
controversial_ideas: Family farms are being sacrificed to score political points.
npcs.perception: I'm talking to the bank about restructuring my farm loan. It's that bad.
npcs.current_plan: Negotiate with creditors and explore alternative income streams.

npcs.name: Fatima Patel
npcs.bio: Fatima Patel works as a hardware store owner and is known around town for being community-oriented.
beliefs: A hardware store is the heartbeat of a working town.
Steel product prices will spike and customers will blame me.
controversial_ideas: I'm stockpiling inventory now before prices jump — everyone should.
npcs.perception: I may need to lay off my only employee. This keeps me up at night.
npcs.current_plan: Consider closing unprofitable product lines to stay afloat.

npcs.name: Diego Hernandez
npcs.bio: Diego Hernandez works as a delivery driver and is known around town for being hardworking and stoic.
beliefs: Drivers see the real economy every day.
If businesses close, there's nothing to deliver.
controversial_ideas: Gig economy drivers have zero safety net when things go south.
npcs.perception: Gas and repairs cost more. Fewer customers. I'm barely breaking even.
npcs.current_plan: Join the drivers' cooperative to share costs and negotiate better rates.

npcs.name: Emma Brown
npcs.bio: Emma Brown works as a taxi driver and is known around town for being cautious and pragmatic.
beliefs: I talk to everyone — the mood in town is shifting fast.
People are scared.
controversial_ideas: Crime goes up when the economy goes down — I see it every night shift.
npcs.perception: Gas and repairs cost more. Fewer customers. I'm barely breaking even.
npcs.current_plan: Join the drivers' cooperative to share costs and negotiate better rates.

npcs.name: Raj Singh
npcs.bio: Raj Singh works as a delivery driver and is known around town for being optimistic and entrepreneurial.
beliefs: More construction means more deliveries.
Tariffs could actually boost local demand.
controversial_ideas: Delivery drivers should unionize before automation replaces us all.
npcs.perception: Gas and repairs cost more. Fewer customers. I'm barely breaking even.
npcs.current_plan: Join the drivers' cooperative to share costs and negotiate better rates.

npcs.name: Svetlana Volkov
npcs.bio: Svetlana Volkov works as a taxi driver and is known around town for being anxious about the future.
beliefs: Everything is getting more expensive.
My car repair costs are linked to steel prices too.
controversial_ideas: If gas and parts keep rising I'll have to stop driving.
npcs.perception: Gas and repairs cost more. Fewer customers. I'm barely breaking even.
npcs.current_plan: Join the drivers' cooperative to share costs and negotiate better rates.

npcs.name: Chen Kim
npcs.bio: Chen Kim works as a delivery driver and is known around town for being quiet but determined.
beliefs: Tech platforms exploit drivers.
Steel tariffs are just one more cost that rolls downhill.
controversial_ideas: Autonomous vehicles will make this debate irrelevant in five years.
npcs.perception: Gas and repairs cost more. Fewer customers. I'm barely breaking even.
npcs.current_plan: Join the drivers' cooperative to share costs and negotiate better rates.
